jokes.text: Can't believe Michael Bay spent a billion dollars of his own money to build that for The Rock.
jokes.setup: Character sees Alcatraz in San Francisco
jokes.punchline: Andy believes Michael Bay personally funded building Alcatraz as a movie set
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - his innocent confusion about reality vs. movie fiction is both endearing and absurd. Well-crafted misdirection.

jokes.text: I can't believe you were able to recite that entire movie, from memory, on the plane ride.
jokes.setup: April's deadpan response to Andy's enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Reveals Andy recited an entire movie during flight
jokes.explanation: Classic April deadpan delivery that reveals more about Andy's behavior. Good character work but not exceptional.

jokes.text: What happens at a National Parks Conference is compiled and emailed to you in a PDF the following Monday.
jokes.setup: Leslie sets up the 'what happens in Vegas' trope
jokes.punchline: Subverts with bureaucratic reality instead of wild partying
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke that perfectly captures Leslie's character - her excitement about bureaucratic processes. Strong structure and highly quotable.

jokes.text: They give tours. / Yeah. That's exactly how they'll be expecting me to try to break in.
jokes.setup: April suggests the obvious solution of taking a tour
jokes.punchline: Leslie treats the normal option as part of some elaborate security expectation
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Leslie's overthinking tendency, treating mundane situations like spy missions.

jokes.text: Wow. I've never worked hard on anything. What a cool life!
jokes.setup: Tom starts to compare this work to previous hard work
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's never actually worked hard and celebrates this fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - his shallow self-awareness and celebration of laziness is both funny and character-consistent.

jokes.text: From now on, everyone call me Kristen. Because I am wigging out right now!
jokes.setup: Tom is stressed about the restaurant opening
jokes.punchline: Makes a pun connecting 'wigging out' to the name Kristen (wig = hair)
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that fits Tom's tendency toward wordplay, though not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: It says 'Tom's bi.' Actually, no, that's good. We're ready.
jokes.setup: Tom points out the unfinished restaurant sign
jokes.punchline: Misreads 'Tom's Bistro' as sexual orientation, then decides it's fine marketing
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag with good misdirection. Tom's casual acceptance of the misreading as good marketing is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Liam Bonneville? The Department of the Interior's resident bad boy?
jokes.setup: Grant mentions Liam Bonneville will be at lunch
jokes.punchline: Leslie describes a parks bureaucrat as a 'bad boy'
jokes.explanation: Great character joke showing Leslie's unique perspective where government officials are rock stars. The oxymoron is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: No idea.
jokes.setup: Ben asks what Gryzzl does after saying they're pitching to them
jokes.punchline: Andy admits he has no idea what the company does
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that reveal Andy's typical lack of preparation. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: So, you can just get your hair cut in the lobby at Gryzzl, huh?
jokes.setup: Tech company has unconventional workspace amenities
jokes.punchline: Andy focuses on the hair cutting instead of the business opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's simple priorities and wonder at mundane things.

jokes.text: we're more like Dayton. But with your help, we can become Toledo.
jokes.setup: Ben admits Pawnee isn't as big as Shreveport or Akron
jokes.punchline: His 'aspirational' comparison cities are equally underwhelming
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation joke where the 'improvement' is still mediocre. Good absurdist logic.

jokes.text: We don't like to say the word 'no'... No. ...So instead, let me say, 'Nah, bro.'
jokes.setup: Tech company claims they don't say 'no' as it's counter-productive
jokes.punchline: Immediately says 'no' then offers equally negative 'nah, bro' as improvement
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of tech company culture with perfect timing and structure. The immediate contradiction is beautifully executed.

jokes.text: Check it out, guys, all done. Only made, like, 12 mistakes.
jokes.setup: Andy finishes cutting someone's hair
jokes.punchline: Proudly announces he only made 12 mistakes
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - his proud announcement of making multiple mistakes is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Dwayne 'The Rock' Swanson.
jokes.setup: Tom addresses Ron
jokes.punchline: Combines Ron's woodworking skills with The Rock's name
jokes.explanation: Decent callback pun combining Ron's stoic nature with The Rock reference from earlier.

jokes.text: Who do you think I am, Thomas Hucker?
jokes.setup: Tom asks for chairs quickly
jokes.punchline: Ron references an obscure furniture maker as if everyone should know him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron joke - his assumption that everyone knows obscure craftsmen shows his expertise and character priorities.

jokes.text: I've never asked you for anything today.
jokes.setup: Tom pleads with Ron using standard 'I've never asked for anything' line
jokes.punchline: Adds 'today' making the timeframe absurdly short
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that subverts the familiar plea. The tiny timeframe makes it both desperate and funny.

jokes.text: I made a poster of you by blowing up your picture from the National Parks newsletter. I cut out your face and made it look like you were dunking over Charles Barkley.
jokes.setup: Leslie admits to having Rebecca's poster on her wall
jokes.punchline: Reveals the embarrassingly elaborate DIY poster creation process
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy that perfectly captures Leslie's intensity and hero worship. The specific details make it both funny and believable.

jokes.text: It wasn't bad.
jokes.setup: Leslie gets excited about Liam reading her proposal
jokes.punchline: Liam's underwhelming response to Leslie's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan that deflates Leslie's excitement. Good character work.

jokes.text: I don't know, I can't... Can I high-five you?
jokes.setup: Leslie is overwhelmed meeting Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asks permission to high-five the First Lady
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Leslie's star-struck awkwardness. The specific request is endearingly innocent.

jokes.text: Huh. I thought he was gonna be on it.
jokes.setup: Andy presumably sends something down a San Francisco hill
jokes.punchline: April's understated disappointment that Andy wasn't riding whatever went down
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan that implies she expected/wanted Andy to do something dangerous. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: What? Why didn't you lead with that?
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions meeting various officials then Michelle Obama
jokes.punchline: Ben's shock that meeting the First Lady wasn't the opening story
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that highlights Leslie's skewed priorities where bureaucrats are as exciting as celebrities.

jokes.text: I don't know! Liam Bonneville has me in a tizzy!
jokes.setup: Ben questions her story order
jokes.punchline: Leslie admits a parks official has her more flustered than the First Lady
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her genuine excitement about bureaucrats over actual celebrities. Great callback.

jokes.text: What is this, a rotten grapefruit? / No, it's my dog's rectum.
jokes.setup: Tom questions the disturbing menu photos
jokes.punchline: Larry casually explains they're pictures of his dog's medical condition
jokes.explanation: Excellent gross-out comedy with perfect Larry incompetence. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Larry. Larry. Larry. Listen to me. This is the worst thing you've ever done.
jokes.setup: Tom discovers the menu mishap
jokes.punchline: Declares this Larry's worst mistake ever (hyperbolic given Larry's track record)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke considering Larry's history of disasters. The repetition of his name adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: It was too perfect. It looked machine-made.
jokes.setup: Tom asks why Ron rejected a chair
jokes.punchline: Ron's impossibly high standards for handmade furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - his craftsmanship standards are so high that perfection is a flaw.

jokes.text: we could tell them that there's buried treasure in Pawnee, and then, after they give us the free WiFi, we tell them that the treasure was love all along.
jokes.setup: Andy suggests how to convince Gryzzl about Pawnee
jokes.punchline: His elaborate con ends with a cheesy friendship moral
jokes.explanation: Great Andy absurdism - his childlike scheme with the wholesome twist is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Every time someone in Pawnee clicks through a slideshow of American Music Award red carpet sideboob fails, they'll say, 'Thank you, Ben Wyatt.'
jokes.setup: Ben describes his WiFi legacy
jokes.punchline: His noble gesture will be remembered through trashy internet browsing
jokes.explanation: Good satire of internet culture with Ben's earnest delivery making the trashy content funnier.

jokes.text: There can't be an alchemist of the Hinterlands. The Hinterlands is a shadow kingdom that can only sustain a provost or a denier.
jokes.setup: Someone claims to be an alchemist in Ben's game
jokes.punchline: Ben corrects them with impossibly detailed game lore
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerd moment - his intense knowledge of his own game creation shows his obsessive personality.

jokes.text: You lost your cityhood?
jokes.setup: Government merger expert reveals the worst possible outcome
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horrified realization of what could happen to Pawnee
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that captures Leslie's worst nightmare - losing Pawnee entirely.

jokes.text: Which is it? Fruity or earthy? It can't be both, they're mutually exclusive!
jokes.setup: Donna describes wine as fruity and earthy
jokes.punchline: Craig's meltdown over contradictory wine descriptors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig intensity over a minor issue. His dramatic reaction to wine terminology is in character.

jokes.text: A man without a palate isn't a man.
jokes.setup: Craig's crisis about his sense of taste
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic declaration about masculinity and taste buds
jokes.explanation: Good Craig melodrama - his tendency to make everything existential is perfectly captured.

jokes.text: Guess I have to start over. Who wants to go look for trees with me?
jokes.setup: Ron breaks a glass during restaurant crisis
jokes.punchline: His solution is to start from scratch by finding trees
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron logic - his solution to any setback is to start from the most basic materials possible.

jokes.text: Yeah, man! We did it! This whole company is ours! / Oh, no, it's not. That's mine. That's mine. It's all mine.
jokes.setup: Andy thinks they won the company together
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately claims sole ownership
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Ben's competitive side emerging after his game victory.

jokes.text: Today we have spaghetti, linguine, fettuccine, ravioli, rigatoni, bucatini, lasagna... Ferrari, Lamborghini, bucatini, lasagna.
jokes.setup: April recites Italian pasta menu items from memory
jokes.punchline: Accidentally lists Italian car brands as pasta types
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that feels natural for April's disinterested delivery. The car brands blend seamlessly.

jokes.text: We had to throw our menus away because they are covered with pictures of Larry's dog's rectum.
jokes.setup: Customer asks for a menu
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact explanation about the dog butt photos
jokes.explanation: Great callback delivered with April's perfect deadpan. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: We just have to apply a salve to his anus every hour for the rest of his life, but... Oh! Enjoy your meal!
jokes.setup: Larry shares good news about his dog's health
jokes.punchline: Graphic medical details followed by cheerful meal wishes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obliviousness - his inappropriate timing and cheerful delivery of gross information.

jokes.text: I mean, people are playing with my cones, babe.
jokes.setup: Ben excitedly describes his game's popularity
jokes.punchline: Accidentally sexual phrasing about his board game pieces
jokes.explanation: Decent unintentional innuendo that fits Ben's earnest enthusiasm about his game.

jokes.text: I wanna stay and go. I mean, how far along are we on human cloning? Wasn't Dolly the sheep like 20 years ago? It's like, 'What the hell, scientists? Get it together!'
jokes.setup: Leslie can't decide between staying and going
jokes.punchline: Blames scientists for not solving her dilemma with cloning technology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie logic - her frustration with scientists for not enabling her impossible desires is both funny and in character.

jokes.text: I love quitting. When I was a kid and things didn't go my way, I would just take my ball and go home. That's better than winning, 'cause then, your friends can't play anymore!
jokes.setup: Ron talks about finishing jobs
jokes.punchline: April's twisted childhood logic about ruining games being better than winning
jokes.explanation: Perfect April dark humor - her sadistic childhood logic is both disturbing and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Well, I hate doing work, but I love being flattered, so maybe I'll give it another try.
jokes.setup: April gives Tom encouragement about his restaurant
jokes.punchline: Tom's honest assessment of his motivations
jokes.explanation: Good Tom self-awareness joke showing his vanity and laziness balanced against his ego.

jokes.text: Because you currently owe me $16,000. For the wood. The labor was free, but the wood was a bargain. At $16,000.
jokes.setup: Ron seems supportive of Tom continuing
jokes.punchline: Reveals massive bill for materials, calling it a 'bargain'
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection where Ron's kindness has an expensive catch. His delivery sells the 'bargain' as deadpan humor.

jokes.text: So, what does your gut tell you? / I'm never gonna be able to decide. I'm gonna be paralyzed by hypotheticals until I die here, in this minivan! At the San Francisco airport.
jokes.setup: Ben asks Leslie to trust her instincts
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dramatic vision of dying indecisively in an airport taxi
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's tendency toward dramatic overthinking. The specific location adds absurdity.

jokes.text: Wait. I'm on Endor. These are the redwoods George Lucas used to create the forest moon of Endor.
jokes.setup: They arrive at redwood forest
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately connects it to Star Wars filming locations
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that shows Leslie's tendency to connect everything to known references.

jokes.text: No, I don't. I'm sorry. That was weird. Okay, bye. / This is Lester Kanopf. / No, you know... Okay. Bye. / Smoothly handled, Lester.
jokes.setup: Leslie accidentally says 'I love you' to Grant on the phone
jokes.punchline: Awkward backtracking and Grant's wrong name reference
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with callback to earlier name confusion. Natural awkwardness.

jokes.text: She wanted to fire me, but she realized that she loved having me around too much, and she couldn't do it.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his emotional reaction
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of what just happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obliviousness - his complete misreading of the situation is both sad and funny.

jokes.text: Did someone call for a party zoo?
jokes.setup: Tom calls for reinforcements
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio's dramatic entrance with made-up phrase
jokes.explanation: Good Jean-Ralphio entrance with his typical over-the-top nonsense phrasing.

jokes.text: All right, so we got a four-way going with my ex, my brother, and this rando? Okay, if that's where the night is going. It's 9:00 in the morning.
jokes.setup: Mona Lisa interprets the restaurant situation
jokes.punchline: Her immediate assumption about group sex, even in the morning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mona Lisa character moment - her casual approach to inappropriate situations is consistent and shocking.

jokes.text: Oh... Now you remember that I got beat up. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Mona Lisa reminds Jean-Ralphio of a past scheme
jokes.punchline: Reveals the scheme ended in violence for her
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor revealing the twins' dysfunctional relationship and past schemes gone wrong.

jokes.text: I wanna go horseback riding! / Deal.
jokes.setup: Craig suggests go-karts
jokes.punchline: Mona Lisa's random counter-suggestion immediately accepted
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist humor showing both characters' impulsive nature.

jokes.text: You're right, the name is too long, I was picturing bigger hats.
jokes.setup: Andy suggests the volunteer group name is too long
jokes.punchline: Leslie's solution involves hat size rather than shortening the name
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie absurdist logic - her solution to length problems involves physical accommodations rather than actual shortening.

jokes.text: Only a moron would ever live anywhere other than Pawnee, Indiana. Leslie Knope, multiple occasions.
jokes.setup: Reading Leslie's statue inscription
jokes.punchline: Ironic quote given that Leslie is leaving Pawnee
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - the quote becomes tragically funny given Leslie's departure. Great dramatic irony.

jokes.text: I ordered a small cheeseburger and both the buns were pizzas.
jokes.setup: Band member describes Pawnee as amazing
jokes.punchline: Evidence is an absurd food combination
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist observation about Pawnee's food culture. The specific image is memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Judge gave me two ankle bracelets. Said it was the first time he ever had to do that. Ja boy's a question on the bar exam! Ya heard!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio mentions house arrest
jokes.punchline: His unique legal situation becomes law school material
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio - his ability to find pride in legal trouble is both absurd and character-consistent.

jokes.text: Judge gave me two ankle bracelets. Said it was the first time he ever had to do that. Ja boy's a question on the bar exam! Ya heard!
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio explaining his house arrest situation
jokes.punchline: Claims his case is so unprecedented it's on legal exams
jokes.explanation: Peak Jean-Ralphio - turning legal troubles into bragging rights. The bar exam claim is perfectly absurd and in character.

jokes.text: Damn, Donna, why you gotta bring the Quackson Five into this?
jokes.setup: Donna threatens to embarrass Ginuwine with childhood stories
jokes.punchline: His nickname for his rubber duckies combining Jackson 5 and quacking
jokes.explanation: Excellent pun that's both creative and reveals embarrassing childhood detail. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: No, just trolling for some daddy. Sorry, I pronounced that wrong. I'm trolling for some dad-dy.
jokes.setup: Tammy explains her presence at children's event
jokes.punchline: Clarifies she's looking for fathers, not engaging in ritual sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good Tammy predatory humor with wordplay twist. Character-consistent darkness.

jokes.text: And I will defeat you right into my pants.
jokes.setup: Tammy threatens to defeat Ron
jokes.punchline: Her victory condition is sexual conquest
jokes.explanation: Classic Tammy line combining aggression with sexuality. Perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And I will defeat you right into my pants.
jokes.setup: Tammy threatening Ron
jokes.punchline: Makes defeating someone sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic Tammy sexual aggression. The phrasing is awkward in a way that fits her character.

jokes.text: Have you lost your mind? / Guys, this is the most important night of my life. Which means it's the most important night of your lives, too.
jokes.setup: Leslie questions Ben's Larry enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Tom's narcissistic declaration about his restaurant opening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom narcissism - his assumption that his importance extends to everyone else is character-consistent.

jokes.text: What? Larry's great.
jokes.setup: Leslie horrified that only Larry wants to come to Chicago
jokes.punchline: Ben's genuine confusion about why that's bad
jokes.explanation: Good Ben moment - his genuine niceness contrasting with everyone else's Larry hatred. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I'll have the 'Joan.' It's a tumbler of gin and it's got crushed aspirin around the rim.
jokes.setup: Joan orders a drink at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Her signature cocktail combines alcohol with pain medication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joan character moment - her drink choice reflects her chaotic lifestyle and substance issues.

jokes.text: I wonder if the carpet is as curly as the drapes.
jokes.setup: Tammy notices Jeremy's curly hair
jokes.punchline: Sexual speculation using carpet/drapes metaphor
jokes.explanation: Standard Tammy sexual humor using familiar metaphor. In character but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Let's get divorced! That way we can get married again! Yes! Let's do it.
jokes.setup: Andy wishing he could marry April again
jokes.punchline: Their enthusiastic plan to divorce just to remarry
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy and April moment - their bizarre romantic logic that somehow makes sense for them. Pure character gold.

jokes.text: It's what I call selfies of other people.
jokes.setup: Tom asks for a 'You-ie' photo
jokes.punchline: Explains his made-up term for photographing others
jokes.explanation: Good Tom wordplay - his tendency to create new terms for existing concepts is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: They're just gonna make each other so miserable.
jokes.setup: Watching Jeremy Jamm and Tammy getting together
jokes.punchline: Ron's satisfied prediction about their mutual destruction
jokes.explanation: Good Ron moment - finding satisfaction in his enemies finding each other. Dark but satisfying.

jokes.text: And by the way, don't think that we are not discussing Duke Silver. When were you going to tell me about that? Unbelievable. I am so furious at you. But I've already forgiven you and you need to teach me how to play the saxophone.
jokes.setup: Leslie discovers Ron's secret musical identity
jokes.punchline: Her rapid emotional journey from anger to forgiveness to demands
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie emotional whiplash - her inability to stay mad combined with immediate practical demands is character-perfect.

jokes.text: When were you going to tell me about that? Unbelievable. I am so furious at you. But I've already forgiven you and you need to teach me how to play the saxophone.
jokes.setup: Leslie discovering Ron played saxophone at the concert
jokes.punchline: Rapid emotional journey ending with demand for lessons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie emotional escalation - anger to forgiveness to demands in seconds. Great character work.

jokes.text: Also, can you bring back Power Rangers? I don't know what it is you do, but you seem important enough to get that done.
jokes.setup: Andy addresses the government official
jokes.punchline: Assumes federal employee can revive canceled TV shows
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - his understanding of government power is charmingly naive and specific to his interests.

jokes.text: Ed, you're a nice guy, but you're the most incompetent person I've ever worked with. And that includes Terry. / Jeez. Get it together, Terry.
jokes.setup: Leslie fires Ed for incompetence
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Terry who agrees with the insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Terry's established incompetence with his self-aware response adding to the joke.

jokes.text: Ed, you're a nice guy, but you're the most incompetent person I've ever worked with. And that includes Terry. Jeez. Get it together, Terry.
jokes.setup: Leslie firing Ed for losing files
jokes.punchline: Casually insults Terry in the middle of firing someone else
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that brings in the Terry callback. Leslie's casual cruelty while being 'nice' is perfect.
